channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Migrate from Dropbox to Google Drive: Simplifying Cloud File Management
item.description: As organizations seek more integrated collaboration platforms, many are choosing to Migrate from Dropbox to Google Drive. While Dropbox remains a popular file-sharing solution, Google Drive offers seamless integration with the broader Google Workspace ecosystem, enabling teams to collaborate,...
item.dc:creator: DavisMiller29

item.title: Dedicated security review command now available in Copilot CLI
item.description: You can now run a security review on your code changes directly from GitHub Copilot CLI. The new /security-review slash command is shipping as an experimental feature in public preview,… The post Dedicated security review command now available in Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Restricting Access is The Most Important Step in a Microsoft 365 Copilot Deployment
item.description: I was asked what the most important step is in the deployment of Microsoft 365 Copilot. It’s a good question. Put simply, restricted access is the answer. That is, restricting Copilot access to information stored in Microsoft 365 locations until your tenant is ready for unrestricted Copilot search...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Office 365 Mailbox Export to PST - Third Party Tools: What’s Your Experience?
item.description: Exporting Office 365 mailboxes to PST is still a common requirement in many Microsoft 365 environments, especially for backup, compliance, and migration scenarios. While Microsoft offers native options like Purview eDiscovery and Outlook export, many administrators also consider third-party tools...
item.dc:creator: KastroMoff

item.title: We Gave Ourselves 20 Minutes to Build an AI Agent for a Lumber Company. The Timer's Still on Screen.
item.description: Here's a confession: most "build with AI" webinars are 60 minutes of slides, 5 minutes of a polished demo someone rehearsed for a week, and a closing CTA. You leave inspired but not really sure what you saw. So we tried something different. We put a visible countdown timer on the screen and gave...
item.dc:creator: JoshuaHuang

item.title: PowerToys 0.100 is here: new Shortcut Guide, Command Palette improvements and much more!
item.description: We’re back with a fresh PowerToys release! This month introduces the brand-new Shortcut Guide, a major Command Palette update with the new Extension Gallery and multi-monitor Dock support, and a wave of improvements to Power Display. We’ve also upgraded PowerToys to .NET 10, improved auto-update...
item.dc:creator: Niels Laute

item.title: Productize, observe, version, and automate MCP servers in Azure API Management
item.description: Introduction As organizations move from AI-assisted applications to agentic workflows, MCP servers are becoming a critical integration layer between agents, tools, APIs, data sources, and enterprise systems. Azure API Management already helps teams bring MCP servers under enterprise governance. But...
item.dc:creator: anishta

item.title: How ARM Tracks Work That Takes Hours
item.description: By Arav Goyal, Joy Shah, Michael Cheng, Manik Sikka, Jenny Hunter, Johnson Shi Introduction When a user creates, updates, or deletes a resource in Azure, the request flows through Azure Resource Manager (ARM) before reaching the service that actually owns the resource. For operations that complete...
item.dc:creator: aravgoyal

item.title: ACI Sandboxes: A Glimpse into the Future of Agentic Compute
item.description: Emerging Technology Preview We are delighted to share innovation in the world of AI. At Build, we demonstrated several innovations that showcase where Azure Container Instances (ACI) is heading and how it can become a foundational platform for the next generation of AI workloads. As AI agents...
item.dc:creator: DanaCozmei

item.title: Dependabot version updates now support the Deno ecosystem
item.description: Dependabot now supports Deno. This applies to version updates, not security updates. Get started Add a Deno entry to your .github/dependabot.yml file, and Dependabot will start opening pull requests on… The post Dependabot version updates now support the Deno ecosystem appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Windows App Update Notification
item.description: Hi everyone,We have deployed the Windows App for a client. Currently, when an update is available, users are seeing an in app banner that says: "Click here to update the app. Meanwhile you can use the app."If the user clicks it, the update finishes successfully. However, our organization requires a...
item.dc:creator: malithamadushan

item.title: Extend Microsoft Purview data protection to AWS Bedrock agents for cross-cloud AI governance
item.description: Organizations are moving fast with AI, and many of those AI workloads are not staying in one cloud. A team might use Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Purview for governance and in addition to Microsoft Foundry they may still choose to run an AI agent on AWS Bedrock or on the Google Cloud Platform. The...
item.dc:creator: Inwafula

item.title: Bring Your Own GitHub App: Connecting Azure SRE Agent to Enterprise Repositories
item.description: What if your SRE agent could access your enterprise GitHub repositories the same way your CI/CD pipelines do with a governed service identity, not a personal token? Azure SRE Agent connects to your GitHub repositories to build rich context about your systems source code, infrastructure definitions,...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
item.description: Our next npm major version, v12, introduces security-related default changes to npm install. All these changes are available behind warnings in npm today on 11.16.0 or newer, so you can… The post Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Power BI June 2026 Feature Summary
item.description: This month, we’re continuing to focus on making every day work a little easier—whether that’s building reports, modeling data, or just getting answers faster. You’ll see progress across Copilot and newer AI-driven experiences, along with a set of practical updates to reporting that help reduce...
item.dc:creator: murray-kp

item.title: Ginkgo Bioworks and Microsoft Discovery: Bringing agentic AI to biological discovery
item.description: Introduction Biological discovery is inherently iterative and non-linear. Progress comes through cycles of hypothesis, experimentation, refinement, and review across data, tools, and teams. At Microsoft Build 2026, Microsoft announced general availability of Microsoft Discovery to support exactly...
item.dc:creator: NihitPokhrel

item.title: Power-up in SSMS 22.7: Schema compare and SQL formatting
item.description: With SSMS 22.7, we're expanding that foundation with two capabilities that database professionals have been asking for in SSMS: graphical schema compare and a built-in SQL formatter. In addition to those key features, we're continuing to expand the SQL projects support in the Database DevOps...
item.dc:creator: drew-sk

item.title: Streaming and Batch Data Architectures with Microsoft Fabric to Azure Databricks
item.description: Author's: Oscar Alvarado oscaralvarado​ and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Note: This article describes a solution idea. Your cloud architect can use this guidance to help visualize the major components for a typical implementation. Use this article as a starting point to design a well-architected solution...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: .NET 11 Preview 5 is now available!
item.description: Find out about the new features in .NET 11 Preview 5 across the .NET runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and more! The post .NET 11 Preview 5 is now available! appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: .NET Team

item.title: .NET and .NET Framework June 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description: A recap of the latest servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework for June 2026. The post .NET and .NET Framework June 2026 servicing releases updates appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Bhandari (MSFT), Tara Overfield

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Claude Fable 5 from Anthropic is now available in GitHub Copilot, the first model in Anthropic’s Mythos class, designed for long-horizon, autonomous coding and knowledge-work tasks. Unlike other Claude models… The post Claude Fable 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Dylan Birtolo

item.title: Reconstructing AI activity in investigations
item.description: Learn how to investigate AI activity in Microsoft 365 Copilot and Azure AI services using a structured, telemetry-driven approach. This playbook helps security teams reconstruct events, assess data exposure, and detect potential threats faster. The post Reconstructing AI activity in investigations...
item.dc:creator: Phillip Misner and Microsoft AI Red Team

item.title: Released: June 2026 Exchange Server Security Updates
item.description: NOTE: Our partners in documentation publishing notified us of an issue that is causing documentation on learn.microsoft.com domain to not show latest documentation version. The issue is being worked on. Microsoft has released Security Updates (SUs) for vulnerabilities found in: Exchange Server...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: Addressing Exchange Server May 2026 vulnerability CVE-2026-42897
item.description: UPDATE June 9, 2026: Please see our release blog post for June 2026 Security Update for more information on this CVE: Released: June 2026 Exchange Server Security Updates | Microsoft Community Hub. On May 14, 2026, Microsoft disclosed CVE-2026-42897, a reported vulnerability affecting Exchange...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: Distributed multi-agent systems with Aspire and Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: Learn how Aspire, Microsoft Agent Framework, and Microsoft Foundry model, run, observe, and publish a distributed multi-agent AlpineAI ski resort demo. The post Distributed multi-agent systems with Aspire and Microsoft Agent Framework appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Tommaso Stocchi

item.title: Microsoft Defender now monitors RPC activity
item.description: Remote procedure call (RPC) is a protocol commonly abused by attackers that allows functions implemented in a separate process, and potentially on a remote machine, to be called as if they were local. Many core Windows and Active Directory capabilities are built on or make use of RPC, which makes...
item.dc:creator: EdanZwick

item.title: From AI Suggestions to Autonomous CRM Actions in Dynamics 365
item.description: 🔷 Executive Summary Most AI implementations in Dynamics 365 start—and end—with case summarization. While useful, summarization alone does not fundamentally transform service operations. In this post, I’ll walk through a CRM Copilot Agent Accelerator built on Microsoft Power Platform that goes far...
item.dc:creator: SachinDas

item.title: From one-off prompts to workflows: How to use custom agents in GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: Custom agents let GitHub Copilot CLI understand your stack and team workflows, turning one-off terminal prompts into repeatable, reviewable processes. The post From one-off prompts to workflows: How to use custom agents in GitHub Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Elevate your telemetry using custom data collection in Microsoft Defender
item.description: At Ignite in November, we announced that Microsoft Defender is now the only endpoint protection solution that allows data-hungry security teams to meet specific telemetry needs by optimizing their data collection right within the Defender portal, without the need to rely on fragmented and siloed...
item.dc:creator: Theo_Cohen

item.title: Copilot Code Reviews for Azure Repos
item.description: Over the last several years, we have encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub to take advantage of the latest AI powered and agentic development experiences that GitHub is delivering. At the same time, we recognize that migrating repositories to GitHub can vary...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem, Andrew Brenner

item.title: OneDrive Office Hours | June 2026
item.description: Get ready for the June OneDrive Customer Office Hours! This session creates space for open conversation around the latest updates and how they support the way you use your files every day. In this month’s session, we’ll walk through what’s new and open the floor for questions, feedback, and shared...
item.dc:creator: sophiapeng

item.title: How to Resolve Intuit Data Protect Has Stopped Working Issue
item.description: The Intuit Data Protect Has Stopped Working issue can disrupt backup processes and increase the risk of data loss. Common causes include outdated software, damaged program files, firewall restrictions, and internet connectivity problems. Identifying and resolving these issues can help restore...
item.dc:creator: Isabellaqb

item.title: Announcing the Path to Production for Agents Webinar Series
item.description: Many organizations have made significant progress exploring AI—building pilots, prototypes, and proofs of concept. Yet a common challenge remains: how do you move from promising experiments to production-ready systems that are secure, scalable, and trusted? Join us for the Path to Production...
item.dc:creator: brauerblogs

item.title: Intune Install Printer Driver
item.description: I am trying to install a Printer driver via a Win32app using System to install. Have set configuration as below: Its a simple powershell script which runs perfectly when installing on a device as an administrator. $printdriver = "PCL6 V4 Driver for Universal Print"C:\Windows\system32\pnputil.exe...
item.dc:creator: tonybap1

item.title: How to Find Inactive (Stale) User Accounts
item.description: Inactive accounts can soak up a lot of paid-for but unused product licenses. With increases for Microsoft 365 licenses due to come into effect from 1 July 2026, it’s time to find and remove unused licenses from inactive user accounts. We discuss two approaches by using the Microsoft 365 Licensing...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Troubleshooting Azure Container Apps and Jobs for .NET and Django Workloads
item.description: Introduction Deploying to Azure Container Apps feels like a huge step forward — you get serverless containers, automatic scaling, built-in ingress, and managed environments without managing Kubernetes directly. But when something goes wrong and your container refuses to start, or your Container App...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: Periodic code scanning of inactive repositories
item.description: GitHub code scanning now supports scheduled security scans of repositories that have had no pushes or pull requests for six months or more. Organizations can use this to maintain continuous… The post Periodic code scanning of inactive repositories appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Security validation for third-party coding agents
item.description: Security validation for third-party coding agents is now generally available. GitHub supports third-party coding agents (including Claude and OpenAI Codex) that work directly within your repositories to implement features, fix… The post Security validation for third-party coding agents appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: I Can't get settings on Windows PC?
item.description: My Windows PC cannot open the Settings app at all, and Windows Update keeps failing on the same update for the second time. Clicking the gear icon does nothing, and the update rolls back after reaching a certain percentage. Please help.
item.dc:creator: Matthewsd

item.title: What to do? For the second time Windows Update that failed?
item.description: I am facing a recurring issue where Windows Update fails to install the same cumulative update for the second time in a row on my Windows 11 PC. The update downloads and reaches a certain percentage like 70 or 90 percent but then throws an error message and rolls back the changes. I have already...
item.dc:creator: Delilahsw

item.title: How to Delete And reinstalling Windows OS
item.description: I want to completely wipe my hard drive and perform a clean installation of Windows 10 or Windows 11 on my computer. I have already backed up all my personal files to an external drive but I am unsure of the correct steps to delete the existing operating system and reinstall a fresh copy without...
item.dc:creator: Su-Jace

item.title: How do you troubleshoot slow boot times after recent updates?
item.description: I’ve noticed my Windows system takes much longer to start after automatic updates. I’ve checked startup apps, disabled some, but it still feels sluggish. What steps do you take first when boot performance drops unexpectedly?
item.dc:creator: Feliox

item.title: Stuck in the insider program
item.description: I seem to be in the insider program with no way of getting out of it. I have contacted support but was met with AI generated responses.I would like some advice on how to get out of the insider program while keeping as much of my data as possible.\Any help would be very greatly appreciated!
item.dc:creator: Tryfecta

item.title: Any source. Any destination. Ready for AI-era.
item.description: Telemetry is exploding, every new app, edge node, and AI agent is a new firehose, and AI has raised the bar on what that telemetry must be: governed, on open standards, observable at agent scale. Today, most teams answer that by stitching together a stack of disconnected tools, each catering to a...
item.dc:creator: shayoni_seth

item.title: What's new in Azure App Service at #MSBuild 2026
item.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, Azure App Service introduced a powerful set of updates designed to help organizations accelerate their journey into AI, without increasing complexity or cost. These innovations focus on one clear business outcome: enabling teams to build, deploy, and scale AI-powered...
item.dc:creator: Mayunk_Jain

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026 recap: vision, launches, and top sessions
item.description: Catch up on Microsoft Build 2026 with the vision lead-off, top developer announcements, and must-watch sessions across the Microsoft developer ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Jon Galloway

item.title: Write Logic Apps in C#: introducing the Logic Apps Standard SDK
item.description: The workflow you always wished you could write in code If you build on Logic Apps Standard, you already know the deal: the runtime is excellent at the unglamorous parts of integration - connecting to systems, retrying, scaling, keeping run history you can actually debug. What you sometimes wanted...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Tools for Azure AD B2C migration now available
item.description: If you rely on Azure AD B2C for customer identity, you’re likely starting to evaluate what comes next. With Azure AD B2C no longer receiving new features and several features recently added to Microsoft Entra External ID, planning your migration is easier and can help you with this important next...
item.dc:creator: NamitaSingh

item.title: Turn insights into signals with Activator as a Business Event Publisher (Preview)
item.description: Activator can now publish Business Events. Turn KPI alerts, threshold breaches, and query results into structured, governed signals your entire organization can discover, consume, and act on.
item.dc:creator: GEGUIRGU

item.title: Analyze Your Business Events in Eventhouse and Real-Time Dashboards (Preview)
item.description: Your business events are now automatically stored and queryable in Eventhouse. Run historical analyses with KQL, build live Real-Time Dashboards, and turn every business signal into actionable intelligence, all without extra configuration.
item.dc:creator: GEGUIRGU
